textMode.description: Text mode allows batch editing of country rules. Format: countryCode countryName priority enabled pattern (space-separated), one rule per line. Saving will synchronize with the database (add, update, delete).

# Country Rules Configuration
# Format: countryCode countryName priority enabled pattern

CN China 100 true (?i)(中国|china|cn)
US United_States 90 true (?i)(美国|united states|usa|us)
HK Hong_Kong 95 true (?i)(香港|hong kong|hk)

dedup.title: Node import deduplication
dedup.fieldDescription: Configure deduplication fields for each protocol. When multiple nodes have identical selected field values, only the first one is kept:

modes.protocol: Deduplicate by protocol fields (current pull only)

alerts.none: Use node content hash for database-wide deduplication to ensure nodes with exactly identical content are not stored repeatedly. This method is not affected by link parameter order, and duplicate nodes across different airports are also filtered.
alerts.protocol: Protocol-field deduplication only applies within nodes from the current pull and does not compare against existing nodes already in the database. Protocols without configured fields continue to use the default content-hash database-wide deduplication.

subscriptionAddress.title: Subscription address settings
subscriptionAddress.subheader: Used to generate subscription URLs for the Telegram bot and web sharing
subscriptionAddress.info: After configuration, Telegram bot and web sharing links will prefer this subscription address. When it is not configured, the web UI uses the current access address and Telegram uses the local address.

field.label: Subscription address
field.helper: Enter a complete address including the protocol (http:// or https://). It will be used as the base address for subscription links after saving.

nodeDedup.title: Cross-airport node deduplication
nodeDedup.subheader: Control whether node content is deduplicated across different airports
nodeDedup.enabledInfo: Currently enabled: nodes with identical configuration across airports (same ContentHash) keep only the first imported copy to avoid duplicates.
nodeDedup.disabledInfo: Currently disabled: each airport keeps its own nodes even if another airport has identical node content. Duplicate nodes inside the same airport are still deduplicated.

globalNodeProcessing.title: Global Node Processing Rules
globalNodeProcessing.subheader: These rules apply to all airports and stack with airport-specific rules
globalNodeProcessing.info: Global rules and airport-specific rules stack together. Filter rules: blacklists and whitelists from both are merged. Rename rules: global rules apply first, then airport rules.

databaseMigration.title: SQLite Data Migration
databaseMigration.subheader: Upload a backup.zip or SQLite database file from an old instance and import it into this instance

alerts.overwrite: This operation overwrites business data in the current instance. Use it only for the first migration into a newly deployed MySQL / PostgreSQL instance, and back up the current instance first.
alerts.recommendBackup: Uploading backup.zip exported from the old SQLite instance is recommended. Uploading a .db file directly migrates database records only and will not restore the template directory.
alerts.exportGuide: To export a backup from the old instance, sign in to the old admin UI, open System Backup from the avatar menu in the upper right, and download backup.zip. The archive includes the db and template directories and is suitable as a migration source.

warnings.title: The latest migration contains {{count}} warnings
warnings.helper: These warnings are also available in the Database Migration task details in Task Center.

options.accessKeysHelper: If old API Keys do not work after migration, make sure the current instance uses the same API encryption key as the old instance, or regenerate them after migration.
options.subLogsHelper: Log data is usually large and is not migrated by default.
options.confirmOverwrite: I confirm that this import will overwrite business data in the current instance
options.restartNotice: After the migration task completes, manually restart the project instance, then sign in again and check the result.

messages.completedWithWarnings: Migration completed with {{count}} warnings. Restart the project instance, sign in again, and check the data.
messages.completed: Migration completed. Restart the project instance, sign in again, and check the data.
messages.taskFailed: Migration task failed. Check Task Center for details.
messages.fileRequired: Choose a backup.zip or SQLite database file first
messages.confirmRequired: Confirm that this import will overwrite current instance data first
messages.started: Migration task started. You can track progress in the lower-right progress panel or Task Center.

countryRulesPanel.title: Country Rules Configuration
countryRulesPanel.subheader: Configure country detection rules for auto-filling node countries.
countryRulesPanel.description:
Country rules are used in the following scenarios:
1. Node Country Auto-fill: Enable 'Country Auto-fill' in airport advanced options. When pulling nodes, the system will automatically parse and fill country codes from node names.
2. Node Naming Rules: When using the $LinkCountryName variable, it will look up the country name based on the country code (e.g., HK → Hong Kong).
3. Country Statistics & Display: Provide accurate country information in node lists, node maps, and other features.

Rules are matched from highest to lowest priority, stopping at the first match.

dialog.countryNamePlaceholder: e.g., China, United States, Hong Kong
dialog.regexHelper: Use regular expression to match node names
dialog.priorityHelper: Higher number = higher priority, same priority sorted by creation time (0-1000)
dialog.testNodePlaceholder: Enter node name to test matching

deleteDialog.title: Confirm Delete
deleteDialog.content: Are you sure you want to delete this rule? This action cannot be undone.

messages.incompleteData: Please fill in all required fields

mode.importDesc: Add new rules, skip existing country codes
mode.replaceDesc: Delete all existing rules and import new ones

format.title: Format Description
format.description: One rule per line, fields separated by spaces:
format.example:
CountryCode CountryName Pattern [Priority] [Status]
Example:
HK Hong Kong HK|Hong Kong|香港 100 enabled
US USA US|United States|America 90 disabled

textField.placeholder:
Enter rules, one per line...
Example:
HK Hong Kong HK|Hong Kong|香港 100 enabled
CN China CN|China|中国 100 enabled

errors.title: {{count}} errors found
errors.invalidCountryCode: Invalid country code (must be 2 uppercase letters)

messages.confirmReplace: Replace mode will delete all existing rules. Continue?

cloudflareTunnel.subheader: Connect the current SublinkPro instance to Cloudflare Zero Trust Tunnel through local cloudflared.

alerts.setup: Create a remotely-managed Tunnel in Cloudflare Zero Trust first, then paste the token from the install command. The system calls the installed cloudflared and passes the token internally so it is not exposed in process arguments.
alerts.notInstalledPrefix: cloudflared was not detected in the current runtime. The official Docker image includes cloudflared; non-Docker deployments must install cloudflared first and make sure the command is available on PATH.

form.autoConnect: Connect Tunnel automatically when the service starts
form.tokenPlaceholderSaved: Leave blank to keep using the saved token
form.tokenPlaceholderNew: Paste the token from the cloudflared service install command
form.tokenHelperPrefix: The token is encrypted when saved or started. It comes from the last segment of the Cloudflare Zero Trust Tunnel install command; see

messages.loadFailed: Failed to load Cloudflare Tunnel status: {{message}}

subStore.subheader: Manage the external Sub-Store sidecar used for expanded subscription output formats.

alerts.sidecar: SublinkPro sends mihomo/Clash bridge YAML to an external Sub-Store service. Native clash, mihomo, surge, and v2ray output remain handled locally. These settings are managed only from this page.

form.baseUrlHelper: Use a private network or loopback URL when possible.
form.timeoutHelper: Saved on this page and used by server-side conversion requests.
form.maxResponseHelper: Maximum allowed response size in KiB. For example, 8192 KiB equals 8 MiB.
form.allowedTargetsHelper: Only selected expanded targets appear in subscription share links and can be converted by /c.
form.bridgeNote: Expanded outputs convert proxy nodes only. Clash strategy groups, rules, DNS sections, and other full configuration sections are not preserved by the Sub-Store one-shot parser.

messages.testSuccess: Sub-Store test succeeded for {{target}} ({{bytes}} bytes returned)

api.testSucceeded: Sub-Store test succeeded for {{target}} ({{bytes}} bytes returned)

401.title: Authorization required
401.description: You need to sign in before accessing this page. Sign in to your account or contact an administrator for access.

403.title: Access forbidden
403.description: You do not have permission to access this resource. Contact an administrator if you think this is a mistake.

404.title: Page not found
404.description: The page you requested seems to be lost in the digital universe. Check the URL or return home to continue.
404.footer: Some paths still lead into the unknown...

500.title: Server error
500.description: The server encountered a problem. Please try again later.

503.title: Service under maintenance
503.description: The system is being maintained and should be back soon. Thank you for your patience.

default.title: Something went wrong
default.description: The system encountered a problem. Try again later or contact an administrator.

webhooks.title: Webhook Management
webhooks.description: Centrally manage multiple notification targets. Each Webhook can have its own method, event scope, and can be independently tested after saving.
webhooks.splitAlert: You can split different event scopes for different notification channels, such as errors, subscription updates, or security notifications. Test requests are not affected by event selection.
webhooks.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
webhooks.emptyTitle: No Webhooks configured yet. It is recommended to create separate receiving URLs by notification purpose for easier maintenance.
webhooks.noEventsAlert: No automatic trigger events are selected. This Webhook will not receive business notifications automatically.

dialog.nameHelperText: Optional. Default name will be shown if left blank.
dialog.urlHelperText: Template variables are supported in URL, e.g., {{title}}, {{message}}, {{event}}.
dialog.headersHelperText: Enter JSON headers, useful for authentication or channel identification.
dialog.bodyHelperText: When Content-Type is JSON, it is recommended to enter valid JSON.
dialog.eventSelectorDesc: Select business events to automatically trigger this Webhook. Test requests will not be affected by this selection.
dialog.infoAlert: Using separate names to distinguish notification channels (e.g., "Error Alerts", "Security Notifications") is recommended. You can send test messages from the list after saving.

handshake.label: Include handshake time in latency
handshake.enabledText: : Measures complete connection time, including TCP/TLS/proxy protocol handshakes.
handshake.enabledDetail: Reflects the real usage experience because every request needs a handshake.
handshake.disabledText: : Warms up the connection first, then measures pure transfer latency.
handshake.disabledDetail: Suitable for precisely evaluating network route quality (excluding handshake overhead). If warm-up fails, the node is considered unavailable.

scope.helper: Group priority is higher than tags: when groups are selected, filter by group first and then by tag; when only tags are selected, filter directly by tag; when neither is selected, test all nodes

scope.tagsPlaceholder: Leave blank to skip tag filtering

dialerProxyDialog.warning: Dialer proxy is only available with Clash-Meta core

messages.started: Check task started in the background

unlock.title: Unlock filter
unlock.emptySummary: Collapsed by default. Unlock filtering is disabled when no rules are added.
unlock.description: Unlock filtering is disabled without rules. Add rules as needed and choose whether all rules or any rule must match.
unlock.orHelper: Any one of the rules can match.
unlock.inactive: Unlock filtering is currently disabled. Add a rule below to enable it.

sort.totalHint: {{count}} items total. Select multiple items to batch sort or move.
sort.groupSortDesc: Manage airport node output order within the same group. Airports placed earlier also output earlier in subscriptions.
sort.selectGroupPrompt: Select a group on the left to manage airport sorting
sort.dragAirportHint: Drag to adjust airport order. Airports placed earlier also output earlier in subscriptions.

rename.clickToAdd: Click variables and separators above to add them here
rename.noTagGroup: No tag groups yet. Create a tag group in Tag Management first.

vars.countryNameDesc: Landing IP country name (from country rules, shows code if not matched)
vars.name: Remark
vars.speedIconDesc: Output an icon by speed result (🟢🟡🔴/❌⏱️⛔️)
vars.delayIconDesc: Output an icon by delay result (🟢🟡🔴/❌⏱️⛔️)
vars.fraudScoreIconDesc: Output a risk icon by fraud score (⚪🟢🟡🟠🔴⚫/⛔️)
vars.duplicateIndexDesc: Empty for the first item in each duplicate-name group, then outputs 1, 2, 3...
vars.tagGroupDesc: Tags in the specified tag group (click to select a tag group)

mobileCard.expandHint: Tap the header to expand details

preview.title: Node Preview
preview.noticeText: This beta feature is not fully stable yet. Preview data is for reference only. The actual result depends on what the client receives, and some clients do not support every related protocol, so node counts may differ.
preview.searchPlaceholder: Search node name, protocol, group, or tag...
preview.emptySearch: No matching nodes found. Try other search terms.
preview.emptyFiltered: No available nodes under the current filter conditions.
preview.footerHint: Showing {{displayed}}/{{total}} nodes · Click a card to view details

nodeTagFilter.title: Node tag filter
nodeTagFilter.description: Reuse node tags to control subscription output, making existing tag rules available for whitelist and blacklist filtering.
nodeTagFilter.priorityHint: Filter by node tags. Blacklist has higher priority than whitelist: nodes matching blacklist tags are excluded first, then remaining nodes must match whitelist tags to be kept.
nodeTagFilter.whitelistPlaceholder: Select whitelist tags (nodes must include one of them)
nodeTagFilter.blacklistPlaceholder: Select blacklist tags (nodes with these tags are excluded)
nodeTagFilter.whitelistHelper: Nodes must contain at least one whitelist tag to be kept.
nodeTagFilter.blacklistHelper: Nodes containing blacklist tags are excluded.

messages.sortModeStarted: Sort mode started. Drag items or use batch selection.

tooltips.copy: Copy this configuration as a new copy

chain.description: Chain proxy rules configure a node's upstream entry proxy. Each landing node matches rules independently in order and uses the first matching rule's outbound configuration.
chain.overlapHint: Tip: if different landing nodes need different outbound paths, make sure each rule's target node configuration does not overlap. Use “Specified node” or “By conditions” to define precise target scopes.
chain.emptyRulesDescription: Chain proxy can configure entry proxies for nodes to relay traffic.
chain.templateGroupPlaceholder: Enter a proxy group name from the template
chain.templateGroupHelper: Enter an existing proxy group name from the Clash template
chain.testUrlHelper: URL used to check node availability. Leave blank to use the default.
chain.toleranceHelper: Latency differences within this range are treated as equal
chain.targetScopeDescription: Choose which nodes this rule applies to

targetTooltips.all: Apply this rule to all nodes

chain.middleNodeDialerProxyHint: All nodes in an intermediate custom proxy group automatically set dialer-proxy to the previous hop
chain.selectOrInputGroupPlaceholder: Select or enter a proxy group name
chain.selectOrInputGroupHelper: Select from the template or enter a proxy group name manually
chain.mobileDialerProxyHint: Nodes inside an intermediate group automatically set dialer-proxy
chain.currentLevelWarning: Current {{count}}-level chain. Latency may be higher.
chain.flowHint: Click nodes to configure them. Hover over a node to quickly delete it.
chain.emptyEntryProxy: Click the button above to add entry proxy configuration
chain.ruleNamePlaceholder: For example: Hong Kong transit to US
chain.mobileDescription: Configure a multi-hop proxy chain: entry → intermediate node → target node
chain.desktopDescription: Click “Add proxy node” to build a multi-hop proxy chain, then click nodes to edit. Keep chains within 3 hops when possible.

preview.title: Chain Preview
preview.subtitle: Subscription: {{name}} | Total nodes: {{count}}
preview.canvasHint: Rules match in order. Each node applies only the first matching rule. Use the mouse wheel to zoom and drag to pan the canvas.

clientUrls.defaultTitle: Clients (click QR code to get address)

dedup.title: Node deduplication rules
dedup.description: Handle duplicate nodes before preview and export. You can deduplicate by a shared field combination or configure fields per protocol.
dedup.commonFieldsHint: Choose fields used to determine whether nodes are duplicates (multi-field combination):
dedup.protocolFieldsHint: Configure deduplication fields for each protocol. Protocols without fields are not deduplicated:
dedup.applyNotice: Deduplication rules apply during node preview and subscription output. When selected field values match exactly, only the first node is kept.

share.title: Share Management - {{name}}
share.empty: No share links yet. Click “Add” to create the first share.

confirm.refreshContent: After refreshing the token, old links become invalid. Continue?

form.name: Share name
form.namePlaceholder: For example: friend access, temporary share
form.tokenPlaceholder: Leave blank to generate a random token
form.tokenHelper: Custom tokens are easier to remember. Leave blank to generate a secure random token.

batch.expireDaysInvalid: Expiration days must be greater than 0
batch.legacyWarning: Includes default share links, proceed with caution
batch.noSelection: Please select shares to operate on

share.noResults: No results found for your search
share.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, token, or share link...

basic.name: Subscription Name
basic.clashTemplateHelper: No available template detected. Please check if the Clash template exists.
basic.surgeTemplateHelper: No available template detected. Please check if the Surge template exists.
basic.updateIntervalHelper: When set to 0, it defaults to 1 day. Currently, besides surge, only a few clash clients support this parameter!
basic.replaceHostTooltip: Replace node server address with the corresponding IP address based on system Host configuration
basic.realtimeUsageTooltip: When enabled, it will fetch the latest usage info (traffic, expiration time, etc.) in real time upon every subscription link access, which increases response time; when disabled, it uses cached data for faster response.

nodeSelection.modeManualHelper: Select specific nodes manually. Nodes will not update automatically with group changes.
nodeSelection.modeDynamicHelper: Select groups to automatically include all their nodes. Nodes will update dynamically with group changes.
nodeSelection.modeMixedHelper: Supports both manual node selection and dynamic group selection.

nodeFilter.maxDelayHelper: Set the latency threshold for filtering nodes. 0 means no limit.
nodeFilter.minSpeedHelper: Set the minimum download speed for filtering nodes. 0 means no limit.
nodeFilter.maxFraudScoreHelper: 0 means no limit; IP quality check must be executed first.
nodeFilter.qualityStatusHelper: Can distinguish between complete results, incomplete info, test failed, not enabled, and untested.
nodeFilter.unlockRulesDesc: Unlock filtering is not enabled without rules. You can add rules as needed, and set whether multiple rules must all be satisfied or any one is enough.
nodeFilter.ruleRelationAnd: Multiple rules must be met simultaneously, suitable for stricter filtering.
nodeFilter.ruleRelationOr: Any one of the multiple rules met is sufficient, suitable for combining multiple candidate unlock conditions.
nodeFilter.keywordHelper: e.g. US, Available, Claude, Region Restricted
nodeFilter.unlockInactive: Unlock filtering is currently disabled. Click the button below to add specific rules.
nodeFilter.countryWhitelistHelper: Select from suggestions or press Enter to add; recommend using two-letter country codes like HK/US/JP.
nodeFilter.countryBlacklistHelper: Select from suggestions or press Enter to add; recommend using two-letter country codes like HK/US/JP.
nodeFilter.residentialHelper: Only complete results show residential/datacenter attribute; incomplete info is kept as a separate state.
nodeFilter.ipTypeHelper: Only complete results show native/broadcast attribute; incomplete info won't be misjudged as native or broadcast.

nameProcessing.builderTooltip: Visual Builder - Drag and drop variables
nameProcessing.templateHelper: Leave blank to use original name, only effective when accessing the subscription link.

advanced.scriptHelper: Scripts run sequentially after querying node data.
advanced.ipBlacklist: IP Blacklist (higher priority than whitelist), disallow specific IPs
advanced.ipBlacklistHelper: One IP or CIDR per line
advanced.ipWhitelist: IP Whitelist, only allow specific IPs
advanced.ipWhitelistHelper: One IP or CIDR per line

hosts.title: Host Management

actions.pin: Pin (pinned hosts are not affected by expiration)

hosts.empty: No Host records yet. Click "Add" to create one.

textMode.description: One record per line, format: domain IP or domain IP # remark. Saving syncs changes with the database (add, update, delete).
textMode.placeholder:
# Example format:
example.com 192.168.1.1
api.example.com 192.168.1.2 # API server
textMode.changed: * Content changed. Save and sync to apply.

messages.required: Domain and IP cannot be empty

search.tagPlaceholder: Search tag name, group, or description...

empty.noTags: No tags yet. Click "Add Tag" to create the first tag.
empty.createTagFirst: Create a tag before adding automation rules

confirm.deleteTag: Delete tag "{{name}}"? Related rules will also be deleted.

tag.helpTag: • Tags classify nodes and can be used for filtering and automation rules
tag.helpGroup: • Tag groups are mutually exclusive. Adding a new tag in the same group removes the previous one.
tag.helpExample: • Example: create “Excellent” and “Poor” tags in the same group, so speed tests keep only the latest rating.
tag.name: Tag name
tag.groupPlaceholder: Select or enter a tag group name
tag.groupHelper: Tags in the same group are mutually exclusive. Leave blank to skip exclusivity.
tag.invalidColor: Enter a valid HEX color value, such as #FF5733

presetDescriptions.speedRating: Classify by speed test result: excellent, good, average, poor
presetDescriptions.latencyRating: Classify by latency: low, medium, high
presetDescriptions.regionCategory: Classify by region: Asia, Europe, Americas, etc.
presetDescriptions.usageCategory: Classify by usage scenario: streaming, gaming, downloads, etc.
presetDescriptions.stability: Classify by node stability: stable or unstable

rule.example: Example: country code equals CN, matching all China nodes
rule.unlockKeywordPlaceholder: Filter by specific service or region
rule.unlockConditionHint: Configure at least Provider or Status

fields.useProxy: Use proxy to download remote rules

placeholders.ruleSource: Enter a URL or choose an ACL4SSR preset
placeholders.proxyNode: Leave blank to automatically choose the best node

helpers.ruleSource: Enter a remote ACL rule configuration URL. Rules are loaded dynamically when generating subscriptions.
helpers.proxyNode: Select any existing node or manually enter an external proxy link. Leave blank for automatic best-node selection.
helpers.includeAllOn: • Enabled: more compact configuration, using client include-all to match nodes automatically. System sorting is not followed.
helpers.includeAllOff: • Disabled (recommended): the system inserts nodes in order and follows system sorting and filtering rules.

confirm.clearContent: Clear all content in the editor?

usage.title: Template is used by subscriptions
usage.message: Template "{{name}}" is currently used by the following subscriptions. Deleting it may affect them. Continue?

baseTemplate.title: {{category}} base template configuration
baseTemplate.description: The base template is the default configuration auto-filled during rule conversion when template content is empty. Changes affect all rule conversion operations that use the default template.

messages.diffSaveBlocked: Currently in AI compare mode. Return to edit mode before saving.

ai.setupGuidance: Go to Settings > AI Assistant to enable and complete configuration.
ai.promptPlaceholder: Tell AI how to adjust the current template...

status.generating: Generating a draft based on the current editor content.
status.outdated: Current content or configuration changed. Generate a new draft again.
status.unsavedTemplate: This template is not saved yet. You can apply it to the editor first, then save.
status.diffReadonly: Compare mode is read-only. Return to edit mode before saving.
status.applied: The editor has loaded the AI draft.
status.revertAvailable: A snapshot before applying is available and can be reverted in edit mode.
status.ready: You can compare, apply, or keep editing the current candidate draft.
status.prompt: Enter an instruction to generate a candidate draft.

messages.generated: AI draft generated. You can switch to side-by-side compare mode.
messages.emptyCandidate: AI generation finished, but no candidate content was returned.
messages.setupUnavailable: AI Assistant is currently unavailable. Go to Settings > AI Assistant to complete configuration.
messages.alreadyApplied: The current editor content already matches the AI candidate
messages.outdatedAccept: Template content or configuration changed. Regenerate or revalidate the candidate before accepting it into the editor.
messages.accepted: AI draft written to the editor. You can keep editing or save directly.
messages.noRevert: No local accept snapshot to revert
messages.reverted: Restored the editor content from before the most recent AI candidate accept
messages.diffSaveBlocked: Currently in AI compare mode. Return to edit mode before saving.

tooltip.partial: The quality API did not return complete fraud score, residential, or IP type details
tooltip.ipv6Partial: The quality API did not return complete fraud score, residential, or IP type details in IPv6 environments

hint.manual: Open GitHub to check releases manually

createDialog.descriptionHelper: Enter a short description to identify this key.

delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this API key? This action cannot be undone.

clearHistory.title: Clear Task History
clearHistory.description: Clear completed, failed, or cancelled task history before the selected time.

trafficStats.title: Traffic Statistics
trafficStats.drillHint: Click a row to view node traffic details under this {{type}}.
trafficStats.detailDisabled: Detailed traffic statistics are not enabled. Enable them in speed test settings.

cronExpressionGenerator.label: Cron expression
cronExpressionGenerator.invalidExpression: The expression format is incorrect. Check your input.

advanced.helper: Format: minute hour day month weekday, for example 0 */6 * * *
advanced.invalidHelper: Format error: minute hour day month weekday

nodeNameUniquify.title: Node name uniquification
nodeNameUniquify.subtitle: Cross-airport prefixes and intra-airport numbering can be enabled independently
nodeNameUniquify.description: Cross-airport prefix uniquification and intra-airport duplicate numbering are independent settings. The former distinguishes same-name nodes across airports, while the latter only handles duplicate names within the same airport.

crossAirport.title: Cross-airport prefix
crossAirport.description: Add a unique prefix to this airport's nodes to distinguish same-name nodes from different airports
crossAirport.placeholderWithId: Leave blank to use default prefix [A{{id}}]
crossAirport.placeholderPending: Leave blank to use the default prefix [Anew-airport-id] after saving
crossAirport.helper: Only applies when cross-airport prefix uniquification is enabled, and distinguishes same-name nodes from different airports

intraAirport.title: Intra-airport duplicate numbering
intraAirport.description: If nodes within the same airport share a name, each duplicate group receives -1, -2, -3... suffixes. Different name groups start from -1 independently.

preview.enabledDescription: When enabled, a unique prefix is added for the current airport. It only affects same-name nodes across different airports.
preview.disabledDescription: When disabled, same-name nodes across airports keep their original names.
preview.intraAirportDescription: Each duplicate group is numbered independently. The Hong Kong and Japan example groups both start from -1 instead of sharing one global sequence.

status.missingDescription: GeoIP database is used for IP geolocation and landing checks. Please download and install it.

download.autoHint: The system detected missing GeoIP data during startup and is downloading automatically. You can stop it, configure a proxy, and download again.

placeholders.proxyNode: Leave blank to auto select the best node

helpers.proxyNode: Choose an existing node or manually enter an external proxy link. When left blank, the system auto selects the best node.

notice.title: Notes:
notice.mmdb: The database must be MaxMind mmdb format and include city data (GeoLite2-City)
notice.required: Without a GeoIP database, IP geolocation and landing checks are unavailable
notice.update: Update the database regularly for more accurate geolocation information

geoIpWarning.title: GeoIP data is not configured
geoIpWarning.description: Some location-related features need GeoIP data to show accurate results.
geoIpWarning.ctaHint: Open settings to configure GeoIP data.

ipDetails.title: IP Details
ipDetails.sourceNotice: Data comes from <ipinfo>ipinfo.io</ipinfo> and <ippure>IPPure</ippure>. Results are for reference only.

nodeNameFilter.title: Node name filter
nodeNameFilter.description: Filter nodes by name. Blacklist has higher priority than whitelist.

nodeNamePreprocessor.title: Node name preprocessor
nodeNamePreprocessor.description: Replace node names before filtering and output.
nodeNamePreprocessor.previewDescription: Preview how the current rules transform node names.

nodeProtocolFilter.title: Protocol filter
nodeProtocolFilter.description: Limit output by node protocol type.
nodeProtocolFilter.priorityHint: Blacklist has higher priority than whitelist: blacklisted protocols are excluded first.
nodeProtocolFilter.whitelistHelper: Only selected protocols will be kept.

starGuide.title: Support Sublink Pro
starGuide.descriptionPrefix: If this project helps you, please consider giving it a Star on GitHub.
starGuide.descriptionSuffix: Your support helps the project keep improving.

feedback.description: Report issues or share suggestions on GitHub.
